hints.text: The **only African country w/ fully BLUE plates**; newer Gen4 plates = European-style white w/ blue left strip
Gen4: **silver or white truck cab** (many region/road-specific variants — see spotlight); black pickup around Dakar & Thiès; sometimes fully blurred
Gen3: car w/ roof rack + **visible RIGHT side mirror** (this exact combo is Senegal-unique) + rifts in the sky
hints.uniq_note: Roof racks & rifts exist elsewhere — the right mirror is the unique part.

hints.text: Drives on the **right** (in covered Africa also GH/NG/RW/TN; Kenya & the south drive left); **French infrastructure**: bollards, poles, signs, road lines
**French** official & dominant on signs — the other covered sub-Saharan countries use English
hints.uniq_note: French + Sahel landscape = Senegal (Rwanda has minor French but looks totally different).

hints.text: White **km markers w/ red top** (road number + distances — the farthest town is usually the biggest); town-entry signs white w/ **thick pink outline** + bold black name
Coverage mostly **west of Touba**; in the east only main roads (map)

**Landscape**: north of The Gambia (+ generally) = flat arid savanna-desert w/ sparse short faded trees; **south of The Gambia** = lusher + palms near the coast (don't confuse w/ green-looking Dakar coastal scrub); **Diourbel** = the only region w/ extensive rural sandy-road coverage (grassier sand near Touba); NW coast = vegetated dunes; slight dry hills → Bakel (→Nayé); greener bigger hills → Kédougou/Mako; irrigated rice → St-Louis↔Richard Toll
**Car meta**: silver car w/ **long front-left antenna** = many roads but never east of Diourbel Region; regular white car = mostly south of The Gambia (+ NE coast + Kaolack↔Saly)

bullets: Divided highways only Dakar↔Touba + Dakar↔Saly (Gen4 only)
N1 Tambacounda↔Nayé: railway parallel (south of the road until Youpe Amadi, then north)
N2 St-Louis↔Richard Toll: hazy, low sun, farmland
N2 Richard Toll↔Matam: wide desert road, cloudless bright blue sky (higher sun = further east)
N3 (Matam region): tall bright-yellow grass patches
NE of Kédougou → Mali border: hazy BUSHFIRE coverage w/ smoke trails, car always blurred
Saloum Delta: flat marshy low vegetation
Kola↔Vélingara dirt road: late-afternoon/evening light (later → west)
Forest of Thiès white road: bright orange dirt, dry brown hills, haze

hints.text: **Car variants by road**: box back-right → Richard Toll↔Louga + N3 Tiourène↔Matam + Mauritania border dirt road; stain back-left → N2 Richard Toll↔Matam + Bakel↔Nayé + N1 Tambacounda↔Nayé; stain + dirty roof → N1 Kaffrine↔Tambacounda + N7 Tambacounda↔Kédougou; **red cloth front-left** → Ouro Sogui↔Bakel; white car w/ antenna DOWN → N6 Ziguinchor↔Kolda; white car w/ dirty back → north of Kolda
**Dakar** = dense capital w/ taller modern buildings; **Touba** = the Great Mosque's five tall minarets visible from afar
